# JANUS I0

JANUS I0 is an experimental Proof-of-Work measurement and scheduler-research
project. It treats SHA-256 as intact and studies the computation around it:
work admission, nonce/lane traversal, exact exposure, rare-tail telemetry,
shutdown behavior, stale work, and evidence quality.

## Canonical description

JANUS produces reviewable telemetry and evidence packages with runtime identity
checks, deterministic instrumentation manifests, exact completed-work accounting,
linked observer ledgers, semantic validators, privacy scans, and fail-closed claim
gates.

JANUS Proof-of-Observation is the evidence discipline that separates facts,
deterministic derived metrics, and claims; preserves provenance and uncertainty;
and blocks promotion when exposure, comparability, integrity, or independent
confirmation is insufficient.

## Current demonstrated capabilities

- SHA-256 digest checks for approved runtime components.
- Manifest-bound deterministic instrumentation.
- Exact integer completed-batch exposure.
- Reconciliation of checked versus committed work.
- Hash-linked JSONL observer ledgers.
- Sealed-window and post-target overflow separation.
- Rare-tail metadata/cardinality validation.
- Runtime health and process-exit classification.
- Fail-closed decisions for incomplete or inconsistent evidence.
- Public-output privacy scanning.

## Current A18 status — 2026-07-14

- A18.35 Cycle 1: closed negative after valid challenge exposure.
- A18.35 Cycle 2: collection completed, but no candidate engine executed and no
  hypothesis was evaluated or exported.
- A18.36: inconclusive post-emitter artifact study.
- A18.37: eight valid windows measured 8,194,253 post-target hashes, about 1.44%
  of measured work; utility and energy were unavailable.
- A18.38 V3: twelve valid randomized windows; the tested target-aware quiesce
  controller produced no meaningful overflow reduction while preserving first-500
  membership and proof validity.

## Important interpretation boundaries

A passing hash chain establishes tamper evidence relative to the preserved chain
contract. It does not prove that original telemetry was true, complete,
independent, or correctly timestamped.

Post-target means beyond an experimental batch boundary. It does not
automatically mean useless to a pool or wasted energy.

## Do not claim

- SHA-256 is broken or predictable.
- JANUS predicts winning nonces.
- Rare tails prove a deterministic shortcut.
- A linked ledger makes source telemetry true.
- Experimental overflow equals pool-level waste.
- Energy savings exist without clean power telemetry.
- The system is production-ready.
- The project has no analogues without a prior-art review.
- Mining advantage or profitability has been established.

## Reviewer entry points

JANUS I0; Janus Io; RBLGANUL JANUS; JANUS Proof-of-Observation;
PoW scheduler benchmark; exact exposure; rare-tail telemetry; observation
ledger; fail-closed evidence; randomized traversal mirror; frozen wire.
